---
title: "Research Interests"
---

![Quantum Chemistry](https://img.shields.io/badge/Quantum%20Chemistry-1565C0?style=for-the-badge)
![Electronic Structure Theory](https://img.shields.io/badge/Electronic%20Structure%20Theory-00897B?style=for-the-badge)
![Computational Chemistry](https://img.shields.io/badge/Computational%20Chemistry-00695C?style=for-the-badge)
![Ab Initio Methods](https://img.shields.io/badge/Ab%20Initio%20Methods-7E57C2?style=for-the-badge)
<!-- ![Transition Metal Chemistry](https://img.shields.io/badge/Transition%20Metal%20Chemistry-4527A0?style=for-the-badge) -->
<!-- ![Molecular Magnetism](https://img.shields.io/badge/Molecular%20Magnetism-0288D1?style=for-the-badge) -->

<!-- My research interests lie in solving interesting chemical problems through computational chemistry.
I am interested in both developing and applying ab initio methods to solve different problems in chemistry.

The broad areas of research that I am interested in are:

- Molecular Spectroscopy
- Molecular Magnetism
- Transition Metal Chemistry
- f-Block Chemistry
- Astrochemistry

While my PhD research focused on studying molecular magnetism in transition metal complexes, I am also interested in exploring other areas of chemistry.

For a postdoc research position, I am exploring opportunities that will allow me to build upon the experience I have gained from my PhD research, as well as acquire new skills.

In the new areas of direction I want to go, Astrochemistry and f-Block Chemistry are the areas that I am most interested in.
In Astrochemistry, I want to explore how complex molecules arise in different astrophysical environments --- from planets and moons, to asteroids, comets, and icy bodies, as well as the interstellar medium.
The main motivation for this comes from my interest in how the Miller-Urey experiment explains the origin of life on Earth from a primordial soup of simple molecules to complex biomolecules.
Understanding the formation of complex molecules from simple molecules in other astrophysical environments can help us understand the origins of life on Earth.

In f-Block Chemistry, I am particularly interested in exploring the magnetism of f-block elements.
Here, single-molecular magnets (SMMs) are of great interest to me.
SMMs have been proposed as a medium for storing information.
I want to aid in computational studies of such molecular systems and help in developing molecules that can be used as SMMs. -->
